revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:炭酸水素塩泉]]
and {{ill|Carbonated Springs|ja|炭酸泉|1=Carbonated Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Carbonated Springs|13=User:Immanuelle/Carbonated Springs|qq=}} are similarly titled and I am unsure whether it makes sense to have the {{ill|Carbonated Springs|ja|炭酸泉|1=Carbonated Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Carbonated Springs|13=User:Immanuelle/Carbonated Springs|qq=}} redirect when that refers to a possibly untranslateable Japanese linguistic distinction[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:55, 12 March 2025 (UTC)|qq=}} [[File:Kamiyu spa 2011.JPG|thumb|Example of a bicarbonate spring, {{ill|Kami-Yu Onsen|ja|上湯温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Yu Onsen|qq=}}]]'''Bicarbonate Spring''' (炭酸水素塩泉, tansan suisoensen) is one of the classifications of {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|qid=Q655311|lt=Onsen|1=Onsen}}<nowiki/>water types based on the displayed spring quality name. It is categorized under {{ill|brine spring|lt=brine springs|ja|塩類泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/brine spring|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 温泉}} among the {{ill|onsen|ja|療養泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/therapeutic springs|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 温泉|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q655311|1=Onsen}} == Definition of Spring Quality == A hot spring containing over 1,000 mg of dissolved substances per kilogram of water, with more than 340 mg of sodium bicarbonate (NaHCO<sub>3</sub>). == Overview == This type of spring contains {{ill|bicarbonate ions|ja|炭酸水素イオン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bicarbonate ions|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 炭酸水素塩|1=Bicarbonate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q409202}}(HCO<sub>3</sub><sup>-</sup>), and analysis often detects bicarbonates as the main component, hence the name<ref group="note">Since bicarbonate ions are also known as "hydrogen carbonate ions," sodium bicarbonate has alternate names like "sodium hydrogen carbonate" or "baking soda." However, for consistency in this article, "bicarbonate" is used.</ref>. Sodium bicarbonate, a representative bicarbonate, is weakly alkaline in solution, and bicarbonate springs often exhibit weak alkalinity as well. Some bicarbonate springs are known as "Beauty Springs" due to their supposed cosmetic effects. While not unique to bicarbonate springs, these springs are believed to have a skin-smoothing effect (see the section on [[#Skin Benefits|Skin Benefits]]). Additionally, some bicarbonate springs, referred to as '''{{ill|Kuroyu|ja|黒湯|1=Kuroyu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroyu|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroyu|qq=}} ''' (black springs), contain dark-colored water<ref>[http://www.soshigaya-onsen21.com/quality/quality_03.html Spring Quality: Unique Traits of Black Springs] (Soshigaya Onsen 21)</ref><ref group="note">Not all black springs are related to bicarbonate springs, such as the black water at {{ill|Shiobara Motoyu Onsen|ja|塩原元湯温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiobara Motoyu Onsen|qq=}} in Tochigi Prefecture, which has no connection to bicarbonate springs.</ref>. Some, like {{ill|Arima Onsen|en|Arima Onsen|qid=Q4790673|lt=Arima Onsen|1=Arima Onsen}} in Hyōgo Prefecture or {{ill|Nagayu Onsen|ja|長湯温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayu Onsen|qq=}}''' in Oita Prefecture, contain high levels of carbon dioxide and are colloquially called '''{{ill|Carbonated Springs|ja|炭酸泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Carbonated Springs|qq=}}''' '''. The term "bicarbonate spring" may appear in the detailed spring quality name even if not included in the displayed name. For instance, a spring classified as a {{ill|Brine spring|en|Brine spring|qid=Q11428612|lt=Brine spring|1=Brine spring}}<nowiki/>might also be described as "sodium-chloride bicarbonate spring" in its technical designation<ref name="senshitsumei26" />. Similarly, a spring classified as {{ill|Chalybeate|en|Chalybeate|qid=Q3046709|lt=Chalybeate springs|1=Chalybeate springs}} might be described as "ferrous (II)-sodium-bicarbonate spring"<ref name="senshitsumei26" />. == Comparison of Old and 11 Spring Quality Names == The following table shows the relationship between old and new spring quality names: {| class="wikitable" |+ Correspondence Between Old and 11 Spring Quality Names<ref name="senshitsumei26">{{Cite web|和書 |title=Reference Material 2: Comparison of Old and 11 Spring Quality Names |url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/docs/senshitsumei26.pdf |format=PDF |publisher=Ministry of the Environment, Japan |accessdate=2018-03-26 }} </ref> ! Old Spring Quality Name !! 11 Spring Quality Name !! Abbreviated Spring Quality Name |- style="background-color:#eef" | Heavy Bicarbonate Earth Spring || Calcium (and Magnesium)-Bicarbonate Spring || Ca(･Mg)-HCO<sub>3</sub> Spring |- | Pure Heavy Bicarbonate Earth Spring || Calcium (and Magnesium)-Bicarbonate Spring || Ca(･Mg)-HCO<sub>3</sub> Spring |- | Bicarbonate Earth Spring || Carbonated-Calcium (and Magnesium)-Bicarbonate Spring || Carbonated-Ca(･Mg)-HCO<sub>3</sub> Spring |- | Salt-Bicarbonate Earth Spring || Calcium (and Magnesium) Sodium-Bicarbonate-Chloride Spring || Ca(･Mg)･Na-HCO<sub>3</sub>･Cl Spring |- | Glauber-Bicarbonate Earth Spring || Calcium (and Magnesium) Sodium-Bicarbonate-Sulfate Spring || Ca(･Mg)･Na-HCO<sub>3</sub>･SO<sub>4</sub> Spring |- style="background-color:#eef" | Bicarbonate Spring || Sodium-Bicarbonate Spring || Na-HCO<sub>3</sub> Spring |- | Pure Bicarbonate Spring || Sodium-Bicarbonate Spring || Na-HCO<sub>3</sub> Spring |- | Carbonated-Bicarbonate Spring || Carbonated-Sodium-Bicarbonate Spring || Carbonated-Na-HCO<sub>3</sub> Spring |- | Salt-Bicarbonate Spring || Sodium-Bicarbonate-Chloride Spring || Na-HCO<sub>3</sub>･Cl Spring |- | Glauber-Bicarbonate Spring || Sodium-Bicarbonate-Sulfate Spring || Na-HCO<sub>3</sub>･SO<sub>4</sub> Spring |- | Salt and Glauber-Bicarbonate Spring || Sodium-Bicarbonate-Chloride-Sulfate Spring || Na-HCO<sub>3</sub>･Cl･SO<sub>4</sub> Spring |- | Earth-Bicarbonate Spring || Sodium-Calcium (and Magnesium)-Bicarbonate Spring || Na･Ca(･Mg)-HCO<sub>3</sub> Spring |} == Benefits == While these benefits do not guarantee effectiveness for all individuals, the following are potential benefits based on the spring quality: === Indications === ==== For Bathing ==== In addition to {{ill|General Indications|ja|一般的適応症|1=General Indications for hot spring use|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/General Indications|13=User:Immanuelle/General Indications for hot spring use|qq=}} , these springs are beneficial for {{ill|cuts|ja|切り傷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cuts|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 切創|1=cut|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4372969}} , {{ill|burns|ja|火傷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/burns|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熱傷|1=Burn|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q170518}} , and chronic {{ill|skin diseases|ja|皮膚病|1=Dermatology|2=ja|3=皮膚病|4=de|5=Hautkrankheit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/skin diseases|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皮膚科学|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q171171}}
==== For Drinking ==== Indications include {{ill|Gout|en|Gout|qid=Q133087|lt=gout|1=Gout}} <ref group="note">Sodium bicarbonate is sometimes prescribed to reduce blood uric acid levels and prevent gout attacks. It works by increasing the pH of urine, thereby reducing uric acid reabsorption in the kidneys and promoting its excretion.</ref>, chronic {{ill|gastrointestinal diseases|ja|消化器病|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/gastrointestinal diseases|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 消化器学|1=Gastroenterology|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q120569}} <ref group="note">For example, sodium bicarbonate may be used as an antacid to neutralize excess stomach acid.</ref>, {{ill|liver diseases|ja|肝臓病|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/liver diseases|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 肝臓|1=Liver|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q9368}} , and {{ill|Diabetes|en|Diabetes|qid=Q12206|lt=diabetes|1=Diabetes}}
=== Contraindications === ==== For Bathing ==== Bathing contraindications include {{ill|General Indications for hot spring use|lt=General Contraindications|ja|一般的禁忌症|1=General_Indications_for_hot_spring_use#General_Contraindications|lt=General Contraindications for hot spring use|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/General Indications for hot spring use|13=User:Immanuelle/General_Indications_for_hot_spring_use#General_Contraindications|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 一般的適応症}}
==== For Drinking ==== Contraindications include kidney diseases requiring fluid or sodium restriction and conditions causing {{ill|Edema|en|Edema|qid=Q152234|lt=edema|1=Edema}} . Drinking is also contraindicated for those with {{ill|Hyperthyroidism|en|Hyperthyroidism|qid=Q16499|lt=hyperthyroidism|1=Hyperthyroidism}}<nowiki/>if the water contains iodine, and for {{ill|Hypertension|en|Hypertension|qid=Q41861|lt=hypertension|1=Hypertension}} if the water is high in sodium. === Skin Benefits === While not unique to bicarbonate springs, their alkaline pH contributes to the "silky" sensation on the skin. Alkaline water can saponify skin oils, creating a soap-like substance. In stronger alkaline springs, it can also dissolve {{ill|epidermal proteins|ja|表皮タンパク質|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/epidermal proteins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} , exfoliating the skin and promoting smoothness, often earning the name "Beauty Springs" (美人の湯). Additionally, springs containing organic substances from {{ill|moor springs|ja|モール泉|1=Moor hot springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/moor springs|13=User:Immanuelle/Moor hot springs|qq=}}<nowiki/>can enhance this effect.
== See Also == * {{ill|Kuroyu|ja|黒湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroyu|qq=}} * {{ill|Moor hot springs|ja|モール泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moor hot springs|qq=}} * {{ill|Carbon Dioxide Springs|ja|二酸化炭素泉|1=Carbon Dioxide Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Carbon Dioxide Springs|13=User:Immanuelle/Carbon Dioxide Springs|qq=}}(pure carbonated springs) == References == === Notes === <references group="note"/> === Sources === <references/>
{{Onsen|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|version=101972111|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:炭酸水素塩泉]]
<!--Bicarbonate springs--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11567142}} [[Category:Hot Spring Types]]
